prerequisites: The email address for that My Hours account (email config field).
The password for that My Hours account (password config field).
A start date (start_date config field) marking the earliest point from which to sync data.

Optionally, a time logs batch size (logs_batch_size config field) controlling the pagination window for time log queries. A pagination of 30 days is a reasonable balance between reliability and speed; reduce it if you have a large volume of team members and time logs.

authMethods.name: Account email and password
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the My Hours API using a My Hours account. Supply the account email in the email field and the account password in the password secret field. No API token or OAuth flow is configured for this connector.

setupSteps.title: Confirm you have a My Hours account
setupSteps.body: Make sure you have credentials for a My Hours account. The connector reads data through the My Hours API using these account credentials.

setupSteps.title: Provide your account email and password
setupSteps.body: Enter the account email in the email field and the account password in the password field. These are the credentials the connector uses to authenticate with the My Hours API.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to the earliest date from which time logs and related data should be synchronized.

setupSteps.title: Adjust the time logs batch size if needed
setupSteps.body: Optionally set logs_batch_size to control the pagination window in days used when fetching time logs. A pagination of 30 days is a reasonable balance between reliability and speed. Lower it when an account has many team members and time logs.

sources.title: Is there an API available? - My Hours Help Center
